description_long: This rifle features a robust, industrial design with two parallel cylindrical barrels running the length of the weapon, connected by three evenly spaced, thick support brackets. The barrels have a segmented, metallic appearance with visible joints and a slightly weathered, dark finish, suggesting heavy use or exposure to the elements. The ends of the barrels are capped and slightly rounded, while the brackets are squared and substantial, providing structural integrity. The coloration is primarily a dark metallic brown with hints of green and red oxidation, giving it a rugged, utilitarian look. There are no visible sights, stocks, or magazine components, and the overall form is minimalistic, focusing on the twin-barrel structure. The rifle's design suggests it may be a specialized or improvised weapon, possibly for urban defense or industrial security.
description: A rugged, twin-barrel rifle with three thick support brackets and a weathered, dark metallic finish. The design is minimalistic and industrial, lacking visible sights, stock, or magazine.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two parallel barrels and three thick support brackets, with rounded barrel ends visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile and the depth of the support brackets, with minimal detail on the barrels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the barrels and the three support brackets, mirroring the front but from the opposite end.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the slim profile and bracket structure.
